data.title: Good Gaming 2025 Form 10-K: Revenue $0, EPS $(0.00)
data.description:
Good Gaming reported $0 in revenue for fiscal 2025 and a net loss per share of $(0.00), reflecting a shift from monetized legacy assets to development of a new title. The company posted a net loss of $(235,674), an improvement versus the prior year driven by lower operating expenses and reduced impairment. Management redirected activity toward developing Galactic Acres and pursuing device-scale distribution partnerships.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $0 (decreased $433 vs 2024, a 100.00% decline)
  • Gross Profit: $0 (gross profit for 2025 not separately stated)
  • Operating Loss: $(231,854) (operating expenses decreased $717,147 vs 2024)
  • Net Loss: $(235,674) (improved $727,289 vs 2024 primarily from lower operating expenses and reduced impairment)
  • Net Loss Per Share, Basic and Diluted: $(0.00) per share; weighted average shares outstanding 129,057,047

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trend: Generated $0 in 2025 versus $443 in 2024 as the company shifted focus away from existing MicroBuddies/Minecraft/Roblox assets.
  • Product Focus: Pivoted development resources to a new title, Galactic Acres.
  • Distribution Strategy: Pursuing pre-installation distribution through ViaOne Services to acquire players at device scale.
  • Operational Plan: Plans include preinstalling games, conducting controlled tests, and scaling deployments to tens of thousands of devices with developer partners.
  • Cost and Activity Reduction: Company materially reduced general and administrative, professional fees, and software development spend during 2025.

data.title: Lucid misses first-quarter vehicle delivery estimates
data.description:
Lucid Group missed expectations for first-quarter vehicle deliveries on Friday, fanning concerns about weakening demand for its luxury sedans amid a broader electric-vehicle market slowdown.

The shortfall highlights the persistent gap between the company's production and its ability to get cars into customer hands, a challenge that has plagued Lucid and other EV startups.

Supply challenges continue to be a concern, Lucid CEO Marc Winterhoff has said, acknowledging that the company was being conservative with its forecast of producing 25,000 to 27,000 vehicles this year, implying growth could top 50%. On Friday, it maintained that forecast range.

In 2025, production nearly doubled to 17,840 vehicles.

Along with a hit from high tariffs imposed on auto parts imports, Lucid, like some of its rivals, has been combating a chip shortage, uncertain supplies of rare earths and a fire in September at an aluminum supplier.

It said deliveries of its electric luxury SUV Lucid Gravity were disrupted for 29 days during the quarter due to a supplier quality issue with second‑row seats, limiting the company’s ability to meet customer demand.

The company said it produced 5,500 vehicles and delivered 3,093 in the quarter ended March 31. Analysts at Visible Alpha had expected Lucid to produce 5,967 vehicles and deliver 5,237 vehicles.

data.title: Lucid Group 1Q Deliveries Disrupted by Supplier Quality Issue
data.description:
By Josh Beckerman

Lucid Group's first-quarter deliveries of the Lucid Gravity were disrupted for 29 days by a quality issue with second-row seats, the electric-vehicle company said.

The company on Friday reported first-quarter production of 5,500 vehicles and deliveries of 3,093 vehicles. It added that the quality issue had been addressed and reaffirmed its 2026 production guidance of 25,000-27,000 vehicles.

Lucid said it would report first-quarter financial results on May 5.

Write to Josh Beckerman at josh.beckerman@wsj.com

data.title: Trump Budget Projects Long-Term Growth Above Many Forecasts — WSJ
data.description:
By Owen Tucker-Smith

The Trump administration's latest budget math assumes faster long-term economic growth than what many economists expect.

The White House on Friday said it sees the economy growing 3.1% a year for the next four years and remaining at 2.9% or 3.0% through 2036. It also sees inflation, as measured by annual changes in the consumer-price index, dropping to 2.2% in 2028 and then staying there through 2036.

The projections, released alongside the administration's proposed budget for 2027, are rosier than expectations among economists on Wall Street and at the nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office. The CBO in February projected growth of 2.4% this year, 1.9% growth next year and 1.8% average growth for the next nine years.

The White House sees gross domestic product rising to $53.15 trillion by 2036, which is $6 trillion above the CBO's estimate.

Other economists' estimates have generally landed closer to the CBO's numbers. Economists surveyed by Blue Chip Economic Indicators in early March expected, on average, annual growth of 1.9% to 2.1% between 2027 and 2037, a far slower pace than the administration's figures. The economists were surveyed in the early days of the war in Iran.

Some forecasts are slightly more optimistic: Economists at the Penn Wharton Budget Model said they expect growth to average 2.1% over the decade.

The president has argued that his agenda, including steep tariffs and strict immigration policies, will boost the economy. But some economists have cautioned that tight immigration limits and aggressive tariffs can weigh on growth.

In a document accompanying the budget, the Trump administration acknowledged the discrepancy between its projection and others, but said not all projections take into account the impact of policies the White House plans to implement in the future.

The growth figures play a large role in the administration's expectations for its balance sheet. Faster growth can mean higher incomes and corporate profits, which generate more tax revenue to help shrink the federal deficit. The difference between 3% and 2% annual growth has major implications for how much revenue the government collects over a decade.

Presidential administrations often project more robust growth than other economists. Maya MacGuineas, president of the Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget, called the new growth outlook "fantastical."

Kent Smetters, faculty director of the Penn Wharton Budget Model, said: "If you had a 1% increase over a full decade, you're going to be talking about several trillion dollars." The White House's projected growth implies "way more revenue than we would project."

Some economists expect the war in Iran — which has rattled energy markets, driven up fuel costs and dragged the U.S. stock market to its worst quarter in nearly four years — to damp growth in the short term. Bank of America lowered its 2026 estimates on Wednesday, flagging risks of "a little stagflation" and noting that high oil prices could threaten consumer spending.

"We expect some long-term economic scarring from the war, including disruptions to global supply chains," the bank said in a report.

Jeff Korzenik, chief economist at Fifth Third Bank, said he found the CBO's estimates somewhat pessimistic, and that a more productive workforce could lead to stronger growth over time.

"The administration's projections would be higher than my own," he said. "But there is a rationale for better-than-expected growth as well."

Write to Owen Tucker-Smith at Owen.Tucker-Smith@wsj.com

data.title: Inside Binance’s Gold And Oil Rush — Are Whales Bracing For A Crypto Shock?
data.description:
Gold (XAU) and silver (XAG) futures have climbed into the top five by trading volume on Binance Futures.

Binance Metal Rush Doesn’t Leave Crypto Behind

Just weeks after Binance rolled out gold and silver perpetual futures settled in USDT, the cumulative volume across the metals contracts already reached the tens of billions of dollars, a CryptoQuant report from yesterday claims.

However, CryptoQuant’s analyst Marteen assures that Binance is still overwhelmingly crypto‑native. Bitcoin leads the futures volume around the low‑$20‑billion range with Ethereum following behind at $18.1B and Solana at a distant third at $3.0B. But the metals’ rise into the top bucket shows non‑crypto assets are no longer a sideshow. Gold is already in 4th place at $2.15B, and silver is right behind it at $1.98B.

Marteen’s conclusion is simple. Binance still leans heavily toward crypto, but it has outgrown being a pure crypto venue. Commodities have soaked up liquidity at speed, and equity‑linked products are now starting to see meaningful flow as well.

Binance Joins The Oil Rush Too

According to WuBlockchain, Binance’s new “TradFi” futures suite (gold, silver and stock‑linked products) has rapidly captured a meaningful share of overall derivatives activity on the platform.

Wu Blockchain
@WuBlockchain

On April 2, the first full trading day after launch on Binance, USDⓈ-margined perpetual contracts for crude oil assets CL and BZ recorded trading volumes of $760 million and $358 million respectively, ranking third and fourth among Binance TradFi perpetual products. Meanwhile,… pic.twitter.com/PoROHzQsur

Apr 03, 2026

Crude oil benchmarks CL and BZ posted volumes of $760 million and $358 million dollars respectively, placing them third and fourth among Binance’s traditional‑finance perpetual products.

Trading activity, however, remains dominated by gold (XAU) and silver (XAG), which together generated $5.58 billion in daily volume, makin up more than 70% of the total. Are Crypto Venues Morphing Into Multi‑Asset Trading Hubs?

Let’s keep in mind that Binance is not the only crypto venue experiencing such a dramatic shift. In recent weeks, Hyperliquid has been under the spotlight for many reasons, but one of the main ones is that the leading perp DEX’s combined HIP-3 (oil, gold and silver) open interest reached all-time highs. The platform is now trading more volume in tokenized commodities than digital assets. Just yesterday, NewsBTC reported that tokenized Brent oil futures on Hyperliquid generated about $46.6 million in liquidations in 24 hours, making oil the third‑most liquidated asset on the decentralized exchange.

Gold and silver have been ripping on the back of inflation worries, rate‑cut bets and geopolitical stress. Binance is joining the 24/7 RWA’s trading hub bandwagon by effectively letting traders express those macro views with high leverage and stablecoin collateral, instead of using legacy commodity exchanges.

Gold and silver breaking into the top five on Binance Futures is a signal that the line between crypto and TradFi markets is dissolving, with liquidity, speculation and hedging all moving onto the same rails.

A portion of derivatives capital rotating into metals and stock‑linked contracts can thin order books and amplify volatility in smaller altcoins during risk‑off episodes.

Sophisticated players might use metals futures on Binance as a hedge against crypto drawdowns. Correlation regimes between BTC and gold (as the one between oil and Bitcoin explained by NewsBTC yesterday) could shift as both trade on the same venue. Ignoring this new macro layer on Binance’s futures board could mean missing an important signal about where “smart” derivatives flow is going.

Cover image from Perplexity. All charts from Tradingview.

data.title: Tamboran farm-in agrees staged earn-in up to ~$28.5M for ~10,000 acres in Beetaloo Basin
data.description:
Tamboran entered a Farm-In Agreement to farm down ~10,000 acres and secure a staged earn-in of up to US$28.5 million.

Key Highlights:

  • Tamboran will farm down ~10,000 acres across Shenandoah North, Shenandoah South and BCDA to Daly Waters Energy.
  • The Farm-In provides a staged earn-in up to ~US$28.5M, subject to structured off‑ramps and a milestone carry to Tamboran.
  • DWE will carry Phase 1 with US$11.6M for Pilot Area work and may carry an additional US$11.6M in Phase 2 for BCDA.
  • Completion is contingent on Tamboran acquiring 98.1% of Falcon Oil & Gas Australia and conditions between DWE and INPEX.

Original SEC Filing: Tamboran Resources Corp [ TBN ] - 8-K - Apr. 03, 2026

data.title: Sharplink Ends Ethereum Asset Management Mandates With Galaxy and ParaFi, No Termination Fees
data.description:
Sharplink said it entered mutual termination agreements on April 3, 2026, with Galaxy Digital Capital Management and ParaFi Capital to end discretionary asset management arrangements tied to the company's Ethereum treasury strategy. Both terminations are effective May 31, 2026, and involve no termination fees or penalties. The company cited the addition of internal asset management personnel as the reason for the change and emphasized there were no disagreements with either firm. Sharplink expects a more centralized, in-house approach to managing its ETH treasury.

Agreement 1: Sharplink to End Galaxy Ethereum Asset Management Agreement; Mutual, No Fees

  • Agreement terminated: Discretionary asset management agreement for Ethereum treasury
  • Counterparty: Galaxy Digital Capital Management
  • Original agreement date: May 30 2025
  • Termination date: May 31 2026
  • Termination type: mutual
  • Reason: Shift to internal asset management personnel

Agreement 2: Sharplink to End ParaFi Ethereum Asset Management Agreement; Mutual, No Fees

  • Agreement terminated: Discretionary asset management agreement for Ethereum treasury
  • Counterparty: ParaFi Capital
  • Original agreement date: May 30 2025
  • Termination date: May 31 2026
  • Termination type: mutual
  • Reason: Shift to internal asset management personnel

Original SEC Filing: Sharplink, Inc. [ SBET ] - 8-K - Apr. 03, 2026

data.title: Mobix Labs Raises $2.55 Million From Leviston via $3.0 Million 10% Convertible Note
data.description:
Mobix Labs entered financing agreements with Leviston Resources, issuing a senior secured convertible note with a $3.0 million face amount for $2.55 million in proceeds. The note carries 10% annual interest from March 31, 2026 and matures on July 31, 2026, with conversion at the lesser of the March 31, 2026 closing price or 85% of the lowest 8-day VWAP prior to and including the conversion notice date. In a default, obligations increase to 125% of amounts outstanding. A related Registration Rights Agreement commits the company to file a resale registration within 14 days and seek effectiveness within 30 days, enhancing liquidity for potential conversions.

Agreement 1: Mobix Labs Enters Securities Purchase Pact for $3.0 Million Convertible Note With Leviston

  • Agreement type: Securities Purchase Agreement for senior secured convertible note financing
  • Counterparty: Leviston Resources
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 31 2026 / same
  • Reason: Strengthen liquidity and fund operations

Agreement 2: Mobix Labs Issues 10% Senior Secured Convertible Note, $3.0 Million, Maturing July 31, 2026

  • Agreement type: Senior secured convertible promissory note
  • Counterparty: Leviston Resources
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 31 2026 / Mar 31 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Through Jul 31 2026 maturity
  • Reason: Obtain flexible financing with potential equity conversion

Agreement 3: Mobix Labs Agrees to Register Conversion Shares for Leviston Under RRA

  • Agreement type: Registration Rights Agreement for resale of conversion shares
  • Counterparty: Leviston Resources
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 31 2026 / same
  • Reason: Facilitate resale registration of conversion shares

Original SEC Filing: MOBIX LABS, INC [ MOBX ] - 8-K - Apr. 03, 2026

data.title: Ooma 10-K: Revenue $273.6M, EPS $0.23
data.description:
Ooma reported fiscal revenue of $273.6 million and diluted earnings per share of $0.23, reflecting a 7% year-over-year revenue increase and a return to profitability driven by business growth and acquisition-related benefits. Net income was $6.5 million, which includes a tax benefit tied to an acquisition, and operating income improved to $4.3 million from a prior-year operating loss. Gross profit was $167.2 million with a 61% gross margin, broadly in line with the prior year.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $273.6 million, up 7% year-over-year.
  • Gross profit: $167.2 million; gross margin 61%, consistent with prior year.
  • Income (loss) from operations: $4.3 million, improved from a $(6.9) million operating loss in fiscal 2025.
  • Net income: $6.5 million, compared to a net loss of $(6.9) million in fiscal 2025 (includes tax benefit related to acquisition).
  • Net income per share: Basic $0.23; Diluted $0.23.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and channel shift: Ooma Business expanded to approximately 64% of total revenue, with business users representing about 49% of core users, contributing to higher average revenue per user.
  • Product and brand momentum: Adoption of AirDial and Ooma Office/Enterprise offerings increased, supporting growth in subscription revenue.
  • Subscription and product margins: Subscription gross margin remained strong at 70%; product margin improved as supply constraints eased.
  • Strategic acquisitions: Acquired FluentStream and Phone.com in December 2025 to accelerate business growth and shift the service mix toward enterprise and channel-focused offerings.

Original SEC Filing: OOMA INC [ OOMA ] - 10-K - Apr. 03, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Kinetik Holdings Files 8K - Entry Into Definitive Agreement >KNTK
data.description:
Kinetik Holdings Inc. (KNTK) filed a Form 8K - Entry Into a Definitive Agreement - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on April 03, 2026.

On March 31, 2026, (the "Closing Date"), Kinetik Holdings LP, a Delaware limited partnership ("Kinetik LP"), which is a subsidiary of Kinetik Holdings Inc., a Delaware corporation (the "Company"), entered into an amendment ("Amendment No. 2 to the A/R Facility") to their accounts receivable securitization facility dated April 2, 2024 and as amended by that certain Amendment No. 1 to Receivables Purchase Agreement, dated as of April 1, 2025 (the "A/R Facility" and as amended, the "Amended A/R Facility") to, among other things, extend the scheduled termination date.

The documentation for the Amended A/R Facility includes a Receivables Purchase Agreement dated as of April 2, 2024 and as amended by that certain Amendment No. 1 to Receivables Purchase Agreement, dated as of April 1, 2025 (the "Receivables Purchase Agreement") by and among Kinetik Receivables LLC, a wholly-owned, consolidated, bankruptcy-remote subsidiary of Kinetik LP, as the seller (the "Seller"), Kinetik LP, as the servicer (the "Servicer"), the persons from time to time party thereto as purchasers (the "Purchasers"), PNC Bank, National Association, as administrative agent ("PNC" or "Administrative Agent"), and PNC Capital Markets LLC, as structuring agent, as amended by Amendment No. 2 to Receivables Purchase Agreement dated as of March 31, 2026 ("Amendment No. 2 to Receivables Purchase Agreement") by and among Seller, Servicer, Administrative Agent and the purchasers party thereto.

Pursuant to Amendment No. 2 to Receivables Purchase Agreement, the scheduled termination date of the A/R Facility was extended to March 30, 2027. In addition, Amendment No. 2 to Receivables Purchase Agreement reduced the facility limit to $225,000,000 and added the ability for Seller to request an increase in the facility limit up to $275,000,000.

The foregoing description of the Amendment No. 2 to Receivables Purchase Agreement is qualified in its entirety by reference to the full and complete terms of the Amendment No. 2 to the Receivables Purchase Agreement, which is included as Exhibit 10.1 to this Current Report on Form 8-K and incorporated herein by reference.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1692787/000119312526142345/d89913d8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1692787/000119312526142345/0001193125-26-142345-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Moody's Ratings Upgrades Moldova, Citing Moves Away From Russia
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Moody's Ratings upgraded issuer ratings for the Moldova government to B2 from B3, as the country has become less dependent on Russia.

The ratings agency said Friday it upgraded the foreign and domestic-currency long-term issuer ratings because Moldova's institutions and governance appear to be strengthening.

Moldovan authorities have demonstrated an improved capacity to formulate and implement policy, Moody's said.

They have also shown a better ability to respond to shocks, specifically by becoming less dependent on Russia for energy supply following the 2022 invasion of Ukraine.

Overall, those improvements have contributed to a reduction on Moldova's political event risk with more resilience against outside attempts to interfere with elections.

Moody's said Moldova still has a high exposure to Russian hybrid attacks and struggles with weak growth performance relative to its peers.

The agency maintained its stable outlook for Moldova.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Crown Reserve Acquisition I to Merge With Carvix in Stock Deal With Up to 50M Earnout Shares
data.description:
Crown Reserve Acquisition I entered into a Business Combination Agreement with Carvix to domesticate to Delaware and merge Carvix into a wholly owned subsidiary, taking Carvix public. Carvix holders will receive 50,000,001 shares at closing and may earn up to 50,000,100 more over four years; the Sponsor may earn up to 3,000,000 additional shares. Supporting agreements include a Founders Stock Letter with lock-up and anti-dilution waivers, a Stockholder Support Agreement securing required approvals, and a planned Investor Rights Agreement for governance and registration rights. Closing remains subject to stockholder approvals, SEC effectiveness, Nasdaq listing, HSR (if required), and a minimum cash condition.

Agreement 1: Crown Reserve Acquisition I to Merge With Carvix in Stock Deal With Up to 50M Earnout Shares

data.title: Firefly Aerospace Expands Revolving Credit Facility to $305 Million; Spread Up 25 bps
data.description:
Firefly Aerospace entered into an amendment to its Credit Agreement to increase the senior secured revolving credit facility by $45 million to a total of $305 million. The amendment raises interest to term SOFR plus 3.25% or an alternative base rate plus 2.25%, and adds a 0.375% fee on unused commitments. The facility matures on Aug 8, 2028. The amendment also removes the minimum free cash flow covenant and sets a $381.25 million minimum liquidity requirement tested monthly.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Amendment to senior secured revolving credit facility
  • Counterparty: Wells Fargo Bank, as Administrative Agent, and other lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 03 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Through Aug 08 2028
  • Reason: Increase liquidity and refine covenant package

Original SEC Filing: Firefly Aerospace Inc. [ FLY ] - 8-K - Apr. 03, 2026
